channel.title: Ari Logan's Blog
channel.description: Ari Logan's IT internship daily blog

item.title: Seventh Day - 7/16/2026
item.description: Most of this day was taken up by a trip to a clinic under a remodel to help redo everyone's desks. It was a good lesson in speed an efficiency when setting up a bunch of workstations with a couple monitors ad a phone. I can spend hours working on my own, but when there are around 20 desks to set up, you can't afford to spend that much time (even with a team).

item.title: Sixth Day - 7/15/2026
item.description: Got experience dealing with phishing e-mails and reports. When reporting a phising e-mail, never forward it to IT or anyone else you think can do something about it. Always use the report function of whatever e-mail client your company uses (usually Outlook). I got introduced to the way my company provndows workstations. It's best to make the process as seamless as possible, and let whatever software is used to manage the devices (in this case NinjaOne) do all the heavy lifting. Updates are simple to perform, but they take up the most time by far.

item.title: Fifth Day - 7/14/2026
item.description: It was kinda a slow day, but I got more good experience taking helpdesk calls and sending e-mails to different departments and how to properly use a shared inbox in outlook. Always make sure to mark e-mails that aren't for you as unread, and CC the shared inbox so everyone in your department can keep up on what you're working on.

item.title: Fourth Day - 7/13/2026
item.description: On my fourth day, I finished all my required trainings, which were mostly related to occupational health safety. The main development is I took my first few helpdesk calls. It's super important for people to save their files in their designated network share, but to make sure they're putting the right types of files in the right places; private files in their private share, but files that need to be shared in a shared folder.

item.title: Week in retrospect - Week of Monday, July 6th, 2026
item.description: For the week of Monday, July 6th, 2026, I was finally exposed to how a real IT department operates. The most important things for IT is repeatability and accountability. That's the benefit of ticketing systems, is a way for IT employees to record their work. And because I am working for a healthcare company, I have also been exposed to the importance of Private Health Information (PHI), which is separate but similar to PII (Personal Identifiable Information). A few other concepts that have been reinforced over the course of my first week are warranties and inventory tracking, trust but verify (always make sure the person is who they say they are and what they want is actually what they need), and decomissioning and lifecycle management.

item.title: Third day - 7/9/2026
item.description: My third day started mostly quiet, I mainly just job shadowed with the other people taking helpdesk calls. I helped diagnose an issue with a training headset, which ended up being caused by bluetooth headsets taking precedence over wired ones. I'm at the point where I can start taking helpdesk calls myself

item.title: Second Day - 7/8/2026
item.description: For my second day, I participated in a couple of site visits where I helped remove an old phone system and re-set up a couple of desks.

item.title: First Day - 7/7/2026
item.description: First day of the internship: got setup with all the software I needed, got a tour of the facilities, and met the team.
